description: colorizeLine() in internal/md/resolve.go applies heading/list styling to lines inside fenced code blocks. Lines starting with # or ## get bold+cyan even when they're inside a ``` fence. The colorize() function processes lines individually with no fence state tracking. Fix: track fenced code block state in colorize() and skip markdown styling for lines inside fences. Render them dim instead. May also want a hidden flag (e.g. --x-render-as tty|markdown|raw) to force writer mode regardless of stdout, for debugging.

comments.text: The TTY renderer also needs to handle nested fenced code blocks properly: outer fences with 4+ backticks should contain inner fences with 3 backticks (per CommonMark spec). The fence tracking in colorize() needs to count backtick length, not just toggle on any ``` prefix.

title: TTY renderer applies markdown styling inside fenced code blocks
